question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the network's inability to confirm transactions to go undetected by flooding the REST API with responses that return HTTP 500/503 (not 404), knowing that shouldRetry() only retries on NOT_FOUND, so all non-404 errors are silently swallowed by onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty()), causing the monitor to never alert on a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a total network shutdown scenario that is masked because clientSubscribe() calls onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty()) after the retry spec is exhausted, meaning all transaction confirmation failures are silently discarded rather than propagated as alerts, preventing detection of a network-wide transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldRetry() only retries on HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND to cause the monitor to stop polling for transaction confirmations when the network returns HTTP 429 (rate limit) or HTTP 503 (service unavailable), effectively blinding the monitor to a network shutdown scenario where the REST API is overwhelmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unbounded flatMap() in clientSubscribe() (no concurrency limit specified) to cause resource exhaustion on the monitor host, preventing it from processing transaction confirmation responses and masking a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the monitor to silently drop all transaction confirmation events by exploiting the directBestEffort() sink in RestSubscription, where tryEmitNext() drops messages without error when the sink has no downstream demand, causing the monitor to miss all transaction confirmations during a network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the subscription to permanently terminate by triggering take(properties.getLimit(), true) to complete the Flux after a finite number of responses, after which no further transaction confirmations are monitored and a subsequent network shutdown goes undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the subscription to silently expire by waiting for take(properties.getDuration()) to elapse, after which the Flux completes and no further transaction confirmations are checked, masking a network shutdown that occurs after the duration window?
